lessonTitle: Stand Still, and See the Salvation of the Lord
quickInsight: When you're completely trapped and there's no way out — God makes ways.

quotableVerses.text: Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will shew to you to day: for the Egyptians whom ye have seen to day, ye shall see them again no more for ever. The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.

Let us be strong like unto Moses; for he truly spake unto the waters of the Red Sea and they divided hither and thither, and our fathers came through, out of captivity, on dry ground, and the armies of Pharaoh did follow and were drowned in the waters of the Red Sea.

And now, my sons, remember, remember that it is upon the rock of our Redeemer, who is Christ, the Son of God, that ye must build your foundation; that when the devil shall send forth his mighty winds, yea, his shafts in the whirlwind, yea, when all his hail and his mighty storm shall beat upon you, it shall have no power over you to drag you down to the gulf of misery and endless wo, because of the rock upon which ye are built, which is a sure foundation, a foundation whereon if men build they cannot fall.

Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.

sections.title: The Story
items: Israel just escaped Egypt, but Pharaoh changes his mind and sends his entire army charging after them. They're pinned — the Red Sea in front, chariots closing in behind. No exit.
God tells Moses to stretch out his hand. The sea splits. They walk through on dry ground while walls of water stand on either side. Pharaoh's army follows and gets swallowed up.

In the wilderness, they hit one crisis after another: bitter water at Marah they can't drink, zero food, zero water. Every single time they panic, God provides — He sweetens the water, rains manna from heaven daily, and makes water pour out of a solid rock.
When the Amalekites attack, Israel wins only as long as Moses keeps his hands raised. When he gets exhausted, Aaron and Hur stand on each side and physically hold his arms up until the battle is won.

sections.title: Why This Matters to You
items: You know the feeling of being trapped — maybe it's a friend group that turned on you, a class you're failing, pressure to do something you know is wrong, or anxiety that feels like walls closing in from both sides. Exodus 14 is Heavenly Father saying: 'I see you. Stand still. I'll fight this one.'

The manna thing is real: God didn't give Israel a year's supply all at once. He gave them exactly enough for ONE day. Your spiritual life works the same way — daily prayer, daily scripture, daily choices. You can't stockpile yesterday's faith for tomorrow's problems.

The water-from-a-rock moment is about the Savior. He IS the rock (Helaman 5:12). He's also the Living Water (John 4:14). When your life feels dry and empty — when nothing seems to be working — He's the source that never runs out.

The Israelites complained constantly, even after seeing miracles. Before you judge them, be honest: when things get hard, is your first reaction to trust Heavenly Father or to say 'this isn't fair'? Nephi's brothers saw the same miracles and still murmured (1 Nephi 2:11–12). Which brother are you being?

sections.title: Talk Starters
items: When's a time you felt completely stuck — like there was no way forward and no way back? What happened next?
Why do you think God made the Israelites gather manna every single morning instead of just giving them a huge supply? What's something spiritual you have to do daily or it stops working?
Moses needed Aaron and Hur to literally hold his arms up. Who in your life holds you up when you're exhausted? Who are YOU holding up?
Honest question: when something goes wrong this week, will your first move be to pray or to complain? What would change if you led with trust?

sections.title: How to Teach It
items: Have a boy hold his arms straight out to the sides. Time how long he lasts (usually under 2 minutes). Then have two friends support his arms — he can go way longer. Connect it to Exodus 17: we literally cannot do God's work alone. That's why we have a quorum.

Bring a rock and a cup of water. Hold them up: 'The Savior is called the Rock AND the Living Water. When everything around you is desert — dry, empty, nothing working — He's both the foundation you stand on and the thing that keeps you alive.'

Ask: what's one thing you do EVERY day without thinking — brushing teeth, eating, checking your phone? Now read Exodus 16:21 — they gathered manna 'every morning.' God built daily habits into survival on purpose. What's your spiritual daily habit?

sections.title: Do This at Mutual
items: Trust challenge: blindfold one person and have the quorum guide him through an obstacle course using only voice commands. Debrief with Exodus 14:13 — sometimes 'standing still' and trusting the voice guiding you is harder than trying to run on your own.

Team relay where one person must keep their arms raised the entire time while the rest complete physical tasks. If the arms drop, everyone restarts. Debrief: that's how the quorum works — we hold each other up, just like Aaron and Hur held up Moses.

sections.title: Challenge for the Week
items: Every morning this week, before you touch your phone, say one prayer asking Heavenly Father for what you need TODAY — not next month, not the future, just today. That's gathering your manna.

Find one person who's struggling — a friend, a sibling, someone in the quorum — and be their Aaron or Hur this week. Hold them up with a text, a conversation, or just showing up. One person, one week.
